news.title: He Blew the Whistle on DOGE. Then His Brakes Were Cut
news.summary: A federal IT staffer filed a complaint about DOGE, then went public. Shortly after Elon Musk boosted a post calling his claims false, his brake lines were cut. Now he’s suing for defamation.

news.title: Meet Microsoft Scout, Your AI Coworker That Never Logs Off
news.summary: Microsoft’s OpenClaw-style agent appears in Teams, just like a human colleague, and automates your dull office tasks.

news.title: Android Is Fighting Phone Scams With a New Feature to Prove Who’s Calling
news.summary: Available for Android 12 and later, the anti-scam feature is baked into Google Dialer, which sends a silent “confirmation signal” to ensure whoever’s calling you is who they appear to be.

news.title: Partiful Is Putting Ticket Payments on Its Platform
news.summary: In the social event planner’s first major move toward monetization, Partiful is getting ticketing directly in the app.

news.title: Flush With Cash From OpenAI, Opal Is Making an AI-Powered Audio Gadget
news.summary: Opal, the company famous for making a fancy webcam, has pivoted to making other consumer electronics. Fueled by big investments from OpenAI and Samsung, it’s working on an audio gadget first.
